note: Barrier-free via tram 22 to Prazsky hrad (2nd Courtyard, no significant stairs); courtyards, gardens, St Vitus & St George accessible; free entry for wheelchair users. Avoid Malostranska/Old Castle Stairs entrance. Some historic buildings & towers have steps.
source: castleofprague.com FAQ; wheelchairtravel.org; Prague City Tourism

note: Main entrance has steps but a ramped alternative entrance exists; interior largely accessible. Inside Prague Castle complex.
Step-free to roll onto, but surface is bumpy old cobbles and it is extremely crowded by day (shoulder-to-shoulder). Cross early morning or at night. Fine for strollers but jostling.
source: nolarolla firsthand; arosieworld; timeout; Barrier-free Czechia

note: Open and reachable but paved with uneven cobblestones; very crowded on the hour at the clock. Navigable with effort; sidewalks bumpy. Clock viewed from square (free).
Streets walkable but cobbled; several synagogues & the Old Jewish Cemetery have steps (Spanish Synagogue has an electric platform lift on request). Museum spread across sites, partial access.

Hilltop park paths mostly flat/accessible once up top, but the funicular is CLOSED for reconstruction until ~summer 2026; getting up is otherwise a steep climb. Tower has a lift to a 20m gallery only. Sources conflict on funicular step-free access.
Large flat park with wide paved paths, beer garden with skyline views, metronome, biking/skating paths; relaxed and family-friendly. Some approaches from the river involve stairs.
Lends strollers, carts and wheelchairs free at the main entrance; a top family pick. Set on rugged riverside terrain so some internal slopes, but well set up for families and wheels.
Relaxed seated way to see the sights from the water; accessible cruise operators exist (check individual boat for step-free boarding). Good low-effort option for seniors/toddlers.
Pretty Baroque quarter below the castle; cobbled, some gentle hills, less crowded than Old Town. Charming but bumpy; Lennon Wall and gardens here.
Broad New Town boulevard-square, smoother and flatter than the medieval core; wide pavements. National Museum at the top.
Recently renovated main building at the top of Wenceslas Square; renovation expected to add full step-free access (elevators). Confirm current lift access on arrival.
Hilltop fortress, park and cemetery (famous Czechs); grounds are wheelchair accessible and great for a picnic/views, though the basilica itself is not step-free.
Outdoor graffiti wall in Mala Strana, viewable at street level; approach streets are cobbled. Little dedicated accessibility info in sources.
